title: Quantum Computers Are Not a Threat to 128-bit Symmetric Keys
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: There is no need to update symmetric key sizes as part of the post-quantum transition, due to the details of how Grover's algorithm scales. Most authorities agree.

title: A Cryptography Engineer’s Perspective on Quantum Computing Timelines
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: The risk that cryptographically-relevant quantum computers materialize within the next few years is now high enough to be dispositive, unfortunately.

title: Turn Dependabot Off
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: I recommend turning Dependabot off and replacing it with a pair of scheduled GitHub Actions, one running govulncheck, and the other running CI against the latest version of your dependencies.

title: Inspecting the Source of Go Modules
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: Code hosts like GitHub don't necessarily show the correct source of Go modules. pkg.geomys.dev is a new convenient viewer for module source.

title: go.sum Is Not a Lockfile
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: In Go, go.mod acts as both manifest and lockfile. There is never a reason to look at go.sum.

title: Building a Transparent Keyserver
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: We apply a transparency log to a centralized keyserver step-by-step, in less than 500 lines, with privacy protections, anti-poisoning, and witness cosigning.

title: The 2025 Go Cryptography State of the Union
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: I delivered my traditional Go Cryptography State of the Union talk at GopherCon US 2025 in New York. It goes into everything that happened at the intersection of Go and cryptography over the last year.

title: Claude Code Can Debug Low-level Cryptography
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: Surprisingly (to me) Claude Code debugged my new ML-DSA implementation faster than I would have, finding the non-obvious low-level issue that was making Verify fail.

title: The Geomys Standard of Care
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: Introducing the set of standards that Geomys maintainers strive to uphold in our professional activity as open source maintainers.

title: A Retrospective Survey of 2024/2025 Open Source Supply Chain Compromises
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: Project compromises have common root causes we can mitigate: phishing, control handoff, and unsafe GitHub Actions triggers.

title: Vulnerability Reports Are Not Special Anymore
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: We needed the insight and confidentiality to protect our users, but now that anyone can get the same results from LLM?

title: Opaque, Interoperable Passkey Records (and a Go API)
author: Filippo Valsorda
summary: Passkey records are an interoperable format for WebAuthn credentials, similar to password hash strings. I propose a potential crypto/passkey Go API based on them.
